programmes.show_description: Pianist James Rhodes explains the role Beethoven's final piano concerto has played in his life. With John Suchet. From 2010.
Christopher Morris attempts to identify a British woman who kept a diary in France during the Second World War. From 2000.
4 Extra Debut. Mary broke up with Philip two years ago - so who is following her around the house now? Starring Maureen O'Brien.
5/5. Will there be a sickening end for our Slimmers' Club killer? What is the future for Alma and Jo? Stars Julie Hesmondhalgh.
3/6. Victoria's plans for a  perfect dinner party go awry. Stars Rosemary Leach, Celia Imrie and Nicola Pagett. From 1992.
6/8. Albert and Harold argue over buying a TV or a car. Adapted from BBC TV, with Wilfrid Brambell and Harry H Corbett. From 1972.
4/7. Mark Steel visits the city of Salisbury in Wiltshire and performs a bespoke stand-up show for a local audience. From 2022.

programmes.show_name: The Secret Diary of Adrian Mole, Aged 13 3/4
programmes.show_description: 5/10. A new family arrive in the street. Absence makes the heart grow fonder for Adrian and Pandora. Read by Harry McEntire.
5/5. After Australia, a surprise dinner invitation comes from an unexpected quarter. Ronnie Corbett concludes his autobiography.
3/6. Scientist Adam Hart-Davis quizzes a panel about himself. With Sue Perkins, Robin Ince, Lucy Porter and Will Smith. From 2006.
4/6. Unlike a rolling stone, school gossip's almost certain to gather moss. Stars Marlene Sidaway and Paul Copley. From 2004.
4 Extra Debut. A once-married couple reminisce about their shared past and compare their current lives. Stars Martin Jarvis and Eleanor Bron.
4 Extra Debut. Hannah's mum is doing her best. However the seven-year-old can't take another meal of baked beans. Read by Tara Lynne O'Neill.
4 Extra Debut. 1960s. A lecturer must re-live childhood memories of a murder. Did he contribute to a miscarriage of justice? Stars Rob Edwards.
After the war, the unsung story of the women who worked in the early American intelligence agency. Read by Nicola Stuart-Hill.
Fosdyke International is going into red cabbage, pigs' feet and maybe even pickled onions. Starring Philip Lowrie. From 1983.
1/6. The shady lawyer stays in a haunted mansion. The Marx Brothers radio shows recreated. With guest Spike Milligan. From July 1992.
6/20. Desert island doings for the 'Swiss Family Horne' - and 'Hornerama' probes the press. Stars Kenneth Horne. From November 1960.

From 10pm until midnight, seven days a week, the Comedy Club has two hours of comedy. Plus Stephen Bailey speaks to comedian, actor and writer Carwyn Blayney.
